channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: STF tem maioria para votação secreta em eleição indireta para o governo do Rio
item.description: The Supreme Court (STF) formed a majority to hold the indirect election for Rio de Janeiro’s governor in secret, with six ministers voting in favor. Alexandre de Moraes and Gilmar Mendes dissented, advocating for a direct election. No majority has yet been formed on the 24-hour incompatibilization deadline, which currently stands at 5 to 1 in favor of 24 hours, while Luiz Fux argues for six months.

item.title: Relator pede prisão de Lulinha; medida depende de aprovação na CPI do INSS e aval de Mendonça
item.description: The rapporteur of the INSS CPI, federal deputy Alfredo Gaspar (PL-AL), requested the preventive detention of Fábio Luis da Silva, known as Lulinha, eldest son of President Lula, based on suspicions of involvement in an INSS fraud scheme. The request still requires approval by committee members, referral to the Senate’s Legal Advisory, and endorsement by Supreme Court Minister André Mendonça. Allegations include influence peddling, money laundering, criminal organization, and passive corruption, with evidence suggesting Lulinha may have received payments through an intermediary linked to a lobbyist under investigation in Operation Sem Desconto.

item.title: O trecho da Lei Antifacção comemorado por procuradores
item.description: The Anti-Corruption Law, signed by President Lula, established the autonomous civil action for asset forfeiture, celebrated by prosecutors. This tool allows the seizure of assets derived from or used in criminal organizations, without requiring a criminal conviction and is imprescriptible. The federal and state governments, as well as the Public Prosecutor's Office, may file such action, which had faced veto concerns but was ultimately retained.

item.title: Moraes propõe eleição direta, mas maioria do STF decide por votação indireta no Rio
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes voted for a direct election to choose an interim governor for Rio de Janeiro, but the majority of the Court opted for an indirect vote via secret ballot in the state legislature. The ruling follows the annulment of the mandates of former governor Cláudio Castro and ALERJ president Rodrigo Bacellar, leaving the post vacant until December 31. Justices also disagreed on the required disqualification period for candidates.

item.title: Crusoé: Lula resgata “fiscais do Sarney” ao botar PF nos postos
item.description: President Lula deployed the Federal Police to monitor gasoline and diesel prices at fuel stations in an operation named 'Vem Diesel'. The move comes amid concerns over the 2026 election and has been criticized for its unfortunate pun on actor Vin Diesel's name. The article likens the action to the 'Sarney fiscal agents', referencing past price control measures.

item.title: CPMI esvaziada, Lulinha no radar e STF em alerta: entenda os bastidores da crise
item.description: The INSS CPMI ended without establishing central political responsibilities, despite detailing a billion-euro scheme of illegal pension deductions, due to interference, leaks, and conflict between Congress and the Judiciary. The name of Fábio Luiz Lula da Silva (Lulinha) emerged as a potential target, but efforts to lift secrecy around his circle were blocked by the STF, reinforcing perceptions of political bias and politicization of the inquiry.

item.title: Eduardo diz que decisão sobre candidatura de Flávio não precisava passar por Michelle: ‘Hierarquia’
item.description: Eduardo Bolsonaro stated that Jair Bolsonaro's decision to nominate Flávio as the presidential candidate did not need to go through Michelle Bolsonaro, defending a hierarchical party structure akin to a military organization. He acknowledged the family rift, noting Michelle's support for Tarcísio de Freitas, but insisted disagreements should be resolved through dialogue. Eduardo and Flávio are attending CPAC in the U.S., hoping to meet Donald Trump, who will not attend the event for the first time in ten years.

item.title: Moraes contraria TSE e defende eleição direta para mandato-tampão no Rio
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes voted for direct elections to choose Rio de Janeiro’s interim governor, contrary to the TSE’s guidance and the expectation of an indirect vote by the state legislature. He argued that Claudio Castro’s resignation was a strategic maneuver to avoid direct suffrage and that direct voting better upholds popular sovereignty amid risks of political capture by criminal groups. His vote marks a turning point in the deliberation, asserting that voluntary resignation should not be treated equivalently to removal for succession purposes.

item.title: Gaspar pede prisăo de Lulinha após ir para PL a convite de Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: Federal Deputy Alfredo Gaspar (PL-AL), rapporteur of the INSS CPMI, called for the arrest of Lulinha, son of President Lula, after announcing his affiliation with the PL, invited by Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), a pre-candidate for president. The move takes place amid rising political tensions between government allies and the opposition.

item.title: Bolsonaro tem alta hospitalar e chega em casa para cumprir pena, usando colete a prova de balas
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro was discharged from hospital after treatment for bilateral bacterial pneumonia and returned home in Brasília to serve his sentence. He was seen wearing a bulletproof vest upon arrival, sparking discussion about his security and the current political climate.

item.title: A pesquisa sobre a disputa Lula x Flávio que ‘pegou o Planalto no susto’
item.description: The presidential race between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro has intensified following AtlasIntel polls showing the latter gaining ground, even leading Lula in some second-round scenarios. This has prompted the government to abandon its defensive stance and launch direct attacks, urging allies to intensify criticism and link Flávio to Jair Bolsonaro’s legacy. Flávio’s rise is attributed to the direct transfer of political capital from his father, surprising even Lula’s allies with the speed of his ascent.

item.title: Lei dispensa multa para contar tempo de trabalho rural anterior a 1991 na aposentadoria
item.description: Law 15.363/26, signed by President Lula, exempts rural workers who performed activities before 1991 from paying fines to count unregistered work time toward retirement. Prior to 1991, RGPS enrollment was optional, making the fine unjust, according to Senator Paulo Paim, the bill's author. The measure benefits those who contributed late or not at all during the optional contribution period.

item.title: O movimento de Flávio Bolsonaro que incomoda a base bolsonarista mais radical
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro’s vote in favor of equating misogyny with racism provoked immediate backlash from the more radical wing of the Bolsonarist base, seen as a departure from core movement priorities. Nevertheless, allies acknowledge the move is part of a calculated strategy to broaden his electoral appeal toward the center, including reducing Jair Bolsonaro’s visibility in campaign materials. The episode highlights the tension between satisfying the loyal base and attracting moderate voters essential for a viable 2026 presidential bid.

item.title: Lula se articula sobre minerais críticos diante de acordos estaduais
item.description: The federal government considers unconstitutional the memorandum of understanding signed by Governor Ronaldo Caiado (GO) with the U.S. Department of State regarding rare earths, arguing that states cannot negotiate the management of strategic resources with foreign governments. The Planalto claims the agreement threatens Brazil’s national strategy on critical minerals and could face judicial challenge, as the Ministries of Mines and Energy and Foreign Affairs were surprised and not consulted. The move is seen as an attempt to lend political weight to the act, bypassing the natural technical interlocutors, such as the U.S. Department of Energy.

item.title: Condomínio de Bolsonaro lança comunicado com restrições a moradores e regras da prisão domiciliar
item.description: The Solar de Brasília condominium, where former President Jair Bolsonaro is serving house arrest, issued a notice to residents with new restrictions, including 24-hour external monitoring, a ban on gatherings within 1 km of his residence, and PMDF patrols. The document warns that any violation of the court order will result in immediate judicial sanctions against residents who authorize unauthorized entry, emphasizing that external security is the responsibility of police authorities.

item.title: A crítica pesada de ator de ‘Três Graças’ à Globo e sua reação imediata
item.description: Enrique Diaz, an actor from Globo's 'Três Graças', posted an ironic image linking Lula to banker Daniel Vorcaro while placing far-right politicians around the Globo logo. He deleted the post within 24 hours, sparking speculation about internal tension at the network.

item.title: Após decisão de Moraes sobre Bolsonaro, uma polêmica volta à tona
item.description: The granting of house arrest to Jair Bolsonaro by Alexandre de Moraes for an initial 90-day period sparked debate over privileged treatment in the penal system and its impact on the STF’s credibility. Though justified on health grounds (recovering from broncopneumonia), the decision reignited concerns about inequality, as thousands of inmates face worse conditions without final judgments. Columnist Mauro Paulino acknowledges the medical necessity but warns the political context magnifies perceptions of judicial bias.

item.title: Veneza Brasileira: a cidade onde dois rios atravessam as ruas e o passeio é eleito um dos melhores do mundo pelo The Guardian e The Wall Street Journal
item.description: Morretes, in Paraná, is known as the Brazilian Venice due to two rivers flowing through its streets and forming small islands in the historic center. The city is also the endpoint of a train ride praised by The Guardian and The Wall Street Journal as one of the best in the world.

item.title: Relator da CPMI do INSS pede prisão de Lulinha por ter deixado país
item.description: The rapporteur of the INSS CPMI, Deputy Alfredo Gaspar (União-AL), requested the preventive detention of Fábio Luís Lula da Silva, son of President Lula, after he left Brazil during a Federal Police operation, citing flight risk. The final report of the commission, over 4000 pages, is being read and will be voted on shortly.

item.title: Conselheiro de Trump publica foto com Flávio Bolsonaro: 'Próximo presidente'
item.description: Donald Trump's advisor Jason Miller posted a photo with Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-SP), declaring him 'our next president' who 'already won'. The post on X (formerly Twitter) shows explicit support for Bolsonaro's pre-candidacy for the Brazilian presidency in 2026.

item.title: CPMI pede indiciamento de Lulinha; PT blinda filho do presidente
item.description: The INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPMI) has requested the indictment of Fábio Luís Lula da Silva, son of President Lula, over alleged involvement in a scheme to divert public funds. The PT is shielding the president’s son, claiming lack of evidence and political motivation behind the investigation. The conflict between opposition and government allies highlights deep political polarization surrounding corruption allegations.

item.title: STF busca saída jurídica para garantir eleições limpas no Rio
item.description: The Rio de Janeiro State Court annulled the election of Douglas Ruas as president of the Alerj for violating TSE directives, following Cláudio Castro’s resignation and Rodrigo Bacellar’s mandate cancellation. STF ministers are seeking a legal solution to ensure clean elections in the state amid a series of political maneuvers, including state secretaries influencing votes without the acting governor’s knowledge. The episode exposes an attempt by Castro’s allies to retain control of the state machine despite his removal for abuse of power.

item.title: Apoio a Eduardo Leite por Pérsio Arida e Armínio Fraga dificulta decisão de Kassab sobre presidenciável do PSD
item.description: Economists Pérsio Arida and Armínio Fraga declared support for Eduardo Leite as the PSD's presidential candidate in 2026, increasing pressure on Gilberto Kassab to choose between Leite and Ronaldo Caiado. Leite positions himself as the sole center representative among the party's pre-candidates, while Caiado has backing from agribusiness and bolsonarist factions.

item.title: Lula e Mercadante devem ir a evento na Espanha de entidade dedicada a combater extrema direita
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva and BNDES President Aloizio Mercadante are expected to attend the inaugural event of the Global Progressive Mobilization in Barcelona on April 16–17, 2026. The gathering, focused on countering the far right, brings together international progressive leaders. The news was reported on March 27, 2026.

item.title: Deltan Dallagnol busca Senado e monta estratégia contra decisão que o tirou das urnas
item.description: Deltan Dallagnol, former coordinator of Operation Car Wash and now a member of the Novo Party, aims to run for a Senate seat in the October 2026 elections, despite being declared ineligible by the TSE in 2022. He claims to be building a political strategy to overturn the decision and return to the ballot. His candidacy emerges amid growing polarization around Operation Car Wash and the anti-corruption agenda in Brazil.

item.title: Conselheiro de Trump publica foto com Flávio Bolsonaro: ‘Próximo presidente’
item.description: Donald Trump's adviser Jason Miller posted a photo with Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-SP) declaring support for his 2026 presidential pre-candidacy, stating he 'already won'. The post followed Flávio's participation in the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) in the U.S., where his brother Eduardo Bolsonaro was also present. Miller further promoted the Magnitsky Act sanctions against Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes, signaling alignment with far-right agendas and confrontation with Brazil's judiciary.

item.title: Veja imagens da chegada de Bolsonaro à prisão domiciliar
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro arrived at his residence in Brasília's Jardim Botânico neighborhood on Friday (27) to begin serving at least 90 days of house arrest, following his release from DF Star Hospital where he was treated for bacterial bronchopneumonia. He wore a bulletproof vest during the transfer from hospital to home, where he will serve a 27-year-and-three-month sentence for leading a coup plot. Images of his arrival were released by Metrópoles/AFP.

item.title: Nova lei amplia uso do microcrédito e permite financiar moradia, saúde e qualificação
item.description: Law 15.364/26 expands the National Program for Productive Oriented Microcredit (PNMPO), allowing an additional credit of up to 20% to finance basic needs such as housing, health, professional training, and mobility, while also authorizing digital service delivery. The law ensures that microcredit operations do not prevent entities from qualifying as OSCIP. President Lula vetoed two provisions: one allowing the CMN to set differentiated interest rates based on funding costs, and another granting special access to FAT resources for non-profit institutions, arguing both would undermine program effectiveness and conflict with existing regulations.

item.title: Tereza Cristina diz estar pronta para ser vice de Flávio
item.description: Senator Tereza Cristina (PP-MS) said she is prepared to be the vice-presidential candidate on Flávio Bolsonaro’s (PL-RJ) ticket in 2026, though the decision is not yet finalized. She emphasized alignment with Bolsonarism, support for policies such as reducing the age of criminal responsibility and ending executive re-election, and advocated for uniting the right to defeat Lula. Official PP support remains under discussion.

item.title: CPMI do INSS: relator propõe ao Senado que acione a Justiça pela prisão preventiva de Lulinha
item.description: The rapporteur of the INSS CPMI, Alfredo Gaspar, proposed to the Senate that the Senate's Legal Advisory request preventive detention for Fábio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha), son of President Lula, over alleged involvement in embezzlement of INSS pensions and retirements, alongside the indictment of over 200 individuals. Lulinha’s defense denies any connection, calling the move politically motivated and asserting he has no direct or indirect link to the investigated facts.

item.title: Flávio aposta em estrategista da campanha de Jair Bolsonaro em 2018
item.description: Marcos Carvalho, strategist behind Jair Bolsonaro's victorious 2018 campaign and founder of AM4, is in final negotiations to lead Flávio Bolsonaro's 2026 presidential campaign. His appointment aims to strengthen narrative coherence and digital sophistication, leveraging his expertise in data-driven and distributed mobilization strategies. Allies confirm the deal is nearly finalized, with an announcement expected at the appropriate political timing.

item.title: Relator da CPMI do INSS pede indiciamento de Lulinha, Vorcaro e mais 217 pessoas
item.description: The rapporteur of the INSS CPMI, Deputy Alfredo Gaspar (PL-AL), presented a report recommending the indictment of Fabio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha), Daniel Vorcaro, and 217 others over alleged improper discount schemes at the INSS that harmed retirees and pensioners. Although the CPMI lacks authority to indict, its final report urges the PGR to investigate and asks the Federal Police to deepen unresolved cases. The commission, active from August 2025 to March 2026, produced a 4,340-page report divided into nine investigative cores, with government and opposition divided over the indictment recommendations.

item.title: Rodrigo Garcia se filia ao Republicanos e vira opção para o Senado em SP
item.description: Rodrigo Garcia officially joined the Republicans party on March 26, 2026, after leaving the PSDB in 2024, in a move aligned with the party's strategy to strengthen its presence in São Paulo. With the support of Governor Tarcísio de Freitas, Garcia is being considered as a potential Senate candidate for the October elections, possibly running alongside federal deputy Guilherme Derrite, contingent on PL's endorsement and Jair Bolsonaro's approval.

item.title: STF: Mendonça e Nunes Marques acompanham Cármen sobre prazo para desincompatibilização no RJ
item.description: Supreme Court justices André Mendonça and Kassio Nunes Marques joined Justice Cármen Lúcia's partial dissent in the vote on rules for the indirect election in Rio de Janeiro, concerning the timeline for candidates' disengagement from public office. The ruling addresses the required period for public officials to resign before running for elective positions, aiming to ensure the legitimacy of the state electoral process.

item.title: Guerra faz diesel subir mais de R$ 1 em 13 Estados, diz ANP
item.description: Diesel prices rose over R$1 in 13 states since the start of the Middle East war, with Bahia leading at a 30.9% increase. The federal government is seeking state opposition support for a R$1.20 per liter subsidy after abandoning an ICMS reduction plan, but faces resistance due to revenue loss and fiscal responsibility laws.

item.title: Lulinha seria “possível sócio oculto” do “Careca do INSS”, diz relatório da CPMI
item.description: The final report of the INSS CPMI states that Lulinha, son of President Lula, may have acted as a hidden partner of lobbyist Antônio Carlos Camilo Antunes, known as the 'Careca do INSS'. Reporter Deputy Alfredo Gaspar requested Lulinha's indictment based on the findings.

item.title: Mendonça e Nunes Marques acompanham Cármen Lúcia sobre prazo de 24h para desincompatibilização no RJ
item.description: Ministers André Mendonça and Kassio Nunes Marques partially followed Minister Cármen Lúcia's dissent, voting to suspend the open vote requirement in Rio de Janeiro's indirect gubernatorial election but maintaining opposition to the 24-hour disqualification period for candidates. Minister Luiz Fux had advocated for a six-month disqualification period and secret ballot, while Cármen Lúcia argued the shortened period is justified by the exceptional dual vacancy and unpredictability of indirect election dates. The STF's virtual plenary judgment remains open until September 30.

item.title: Prisão domiciliar de Bolsonaro gera expectativa por relação melhor com o STF; veja bastidores
item.description: The house arrest of Jair Bolsonaro, granted by Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes, has sparked expectations of improved relations between Bolsonaro's allies and the judiciary, interpreted as a sign of the Court's weakening following the Banco Master scandal. Government officials see it as a chance to ease tensions, while Bolsonaro supporters highlight the moderate rhetoric of Senator Flávio Bolsonaro and the meeting between Michelle Bolsonaro and Moraes as key to the rapprochement, though non-negotiable demands remain regarding the detention conditions and penalties.

item.title: Em relatório final, CPMI do INSS defende prisão preventiva de Lulinha
item.description: The INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPMI) concluded its investigation and recommended the preventive detention of Fábio Luís Lula da Silva, known as Lulinha, for alleged involvement in a criminal organization and attempts to evade justice by traveling to Spain during a Federal Police operation. The over 4,000-page report cites undue benefits received and evidence of flight to avoid legal accountability. The CPMI is set to close on Saturday, the 28th, after the Supreme Federal Court (STF) rejected a request to extend its mandate.

item.title: SP investe cerca de R$ 34 bilhões por ano em infraestrutura, diz Tarcísio
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas stated that São Paulo state invests between R$32 billion and R$34 billion annually in infrastructure, equivalent to 11% of the state’s net revenue. He emphasized that these investments are made through public-private partnerships, with nearly R$394 billion in PPP contracts already signed — surpassing the initial target of R$220 billion. He also noted the imminent inauguration of Line 17-gold of the metro and reduced water tariffs via Sabesp dividends directed to the Universalization of Sanitation Fund.

item.title: Líder do PL diz que entrará com queixa-crime contra Gilmar
item.description: PL Chamber leader Sóstenes Cavalcante said he will file a criminal complaint against STF Justice Gilmar Mendes after Mendes accused members of the INSS CPMI of leaking data from Master founder Daniel Vorcaro. Cavalcante denied any evidence of leaks by lawmakers and demanded Mendes provide proof and identify the source. The leak involved 1.08 GB of data from Vorcaro’s phones seized by the PF, including 221 files (329.3 MB) that were disclosed, such as personal messages with his ex-girlfriend.

item.title: Condomínio de Bolsonaro impõe restrições após domiciliar; leia as regras
item.description: The Solar de Brasília condominium, where former President Jair Bolsonaro is serving temporary house arrest, issued rules to residents following a judicial decision by Minister Alexandre de Moraes. The measures include continuous monitoring by the DF Military Police, a ban on protests and gatherings within a 1 km radius, strict visitor control, and emphasis on residents' legal responsibility for any violations. The administration reiterates its partisan neutrality and states that external security is solely the responsibility of law enforcement.

item.title: Delação de Camisotti entra na reta final e deve selar destino do Careca do INSS e políticos
item.description: The plea bargain of Mauricio Camisotti, a businessman involved in the billion-dollar INSS fraud scheme, is in its final stage and expected to be homologated in April. His testimonies detail the bribery system involving Antônio Carlos Camilo, known as 'Careca do INSS', implicate high-ranking Lula government officials, INSS servants, and congressmen, and mention Roberta Luchsinger, a friend of Lula’s son. The scheme diverted over R$6 billion from more than five million retirees, and Camisotti’s deal closes the door to future plea bargains, having already exposed the full political machinery behind the fraud.

item.title: CPMI do INSS: Gaspar pede indiciamento de Lulinha
item.description: The rapporteur of the INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPMI), Deputy Alfredo Gaspar (União-LA), requested in his final report the indictment of Fábio Luís Lula da Silva, known as Lulinha, son of President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, for alleged involvement in a scheme to divert pensions and retirement benefits. The accusation is based on investigations indicating his participation in pension fraud.

item.title: Petistas pedem ao STF prisão domiciliar para maiores de 70 anos
item.description: Federal deputies from the Workers' Party (PT) filed a collective habeas corpus with the STF requesting house arrest for inmates over 70 or suffering from serious illnesses, following the decision that benefited Jair Bolsonaro, 71, allowing him to serve his sentence at home. Lawmakers argue it is unacceptable for the state to expose detainees to health risks due to inadequate medical care in prisons.

item.title: Governador do Ceará, do PT, destaca contas equilibradas e redução da dívida do estado
item.description: Ceará Governor Elmano de Freitas (PT) highlighted balanced state finances, a reduction in state debt from 29% to 26% of revenues, and a top A+ credit rating (Capag), alongside record public investments of R$ 4.8 billion in 2025. He emphasized that fiscal rigor enables greater public investment and policies, contrasting with the federal government's persistent deficits and rising public debt.

item.title: Saiba em quais Estados o diesel está mais caro
item.description: Diesel prices rose by at least R$ 1 in 13 states since the start of the Middle East war, with Bahia recording the highest increase (R$ 1.85, +30.9%). The federal government proposes a R$ 1.20 per liter subsidy, split between the Union and states, to counter the rise, but its effectiveness hinges on opposition-led states like São Paulo and Paraná — which together accounted for 42% of Brazil’s diesel imports in 2025 — joining the initiative.

item.title: Bolsonaro recibe el alta hospitalaria y vuelve a casa para cumplir una pena de 27 años
item.description: Jair Bolsonaro was discharged from the hospital after a two-week stay for acute pneumonia and returned to his residence in Brasília to serve, under house arrest, a 27-year sentence for coup-related offenses. For the next 90 days, he must wear an electronic ankle monitor and is banned from using social media, cell phones, recording audio or video, and receiving unrestricted visits, as ordered by Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes. After this period, the judge will reassess his detention conditions.

item.title: Crusoé: Relatório governista da CPMI do INSS não cita Lula
item.description: The governing coalition's report from the INSS CPMI, obtained by O Antagonista, deliberately omits any mention of President Lula to shield him during his 2026 re-election bid. The fifteen-page document avoids linking the government to the INSS investigations, preserving a neutral narrative around the president.

item.title: Bolsonaro deixa hospital e cumpre prisão domiciliar em casa
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro was discharged from the hospital after two weeks of treatment for bronchopneumonia and transferred to his residence in Brasília, where he will serve his sentence under house arrest. He had been hospitalized due to respiratory complications but now continues his legal process at home.
